Set 1.text: All Faldos are Grondles. All Grondles are Quirns. Are all Faldos definitely Plimbs?
What comes next in the pattern? C, G, K, O, ___?
What comes next? 7, 9, 12, 16, 21, ___?
What position is Sofia in?

media.html:
Ravi, Sofia, Tom are arranged in a line, positions 1 to 3 (left to right).

  • Tom is NOT next to Ravi.
  • Tom is somewhere before Ravi.
  • Sofia is next to Tom.

Set 2.text: All Blyphs are Marbits. All Marbits are Grondles. Are all Blyphs definitely Grondles?
What comes next in the pattern? D, F, H, J, ___?
What comes next? 3, 8, 14, 21, 29, ___?
Who is in position 2?

media.html:
Zara, Maya, Noah are arranged in a line, positions 1 to 3 (left to right).

  • Maya is somewhere before Zara.
  • Zara is somewhere before Noah.
  • Maya is immediately before Zara.

Set 2.text: Which word completes ALL three: BACK___, COURT___, VINE___? They should form real words when joined.

media.html:

  • Maya is next to Zara.
  • Maya is immediately before Zara.
  • Noah is immediately before Maya.

What position is Anya in?

media.html:
Carla, Ben, Anya are arranged in a line, positions 1 to 3 (left to right).

  • Anya is immediately before Carla.
  • Carla is next to Anya.
  • Carla is somewhere before Ben.

What comes next? 8, 11, 15, 20, 26, ___?
What position is Priya in?

media.html:
Priya, Omar, Kate are arranged in a line, positions 1 to 3 (left to right).

  • Priya is next to Kate.
  • Priya is somewhere before Kate.
  • Omar is immediately before Priya.

media.html:

  • Kate is next to Omar.
  • Omar is somewhere before Priya.

Set 7.text: All Plimbs are Wexits. All Wexits are Faldos. Are all Plimbs definitely Faldos?
What comes next in the pattern? C, F, I, L, ___?
What comes next? 2, 5, 9, 14, 20, ___?
What position is Kate in?

media.html:
Kate, Priya, Omar are arranged in a line, positions 1 to 3 (left to right).

  • Kate is next to Priya.
  • Omar is immediately before Priya.
  • Kate is NOT next to Omar.

What position is Omar in?

media.html:
Kate, Priya, Omar are arranged in a line, positions 1 to 3 (left to right).

  • Omar is next to Kate.
  • Priya is next to Kate.
  • Priya is somewhere before Kate.

media.html:
Priya, Kate, Omar are arranged in a line, positions 1 to 3 (left to right).

  • Kate is somewhere before Omar.
  • Omar is NOT next to Kate.
  • Priya is next to Kate.

Set 10.text: All Blims are Zorbs. All Zorbs are Trebs. Are all Blims definitely Trebs?
What comes next in the pattern? F, J, N, R, ___?
What comes next? 6, 11, 17, 24, 32, ___?
Who is in position 2?

media.html:
Noah, Maya, Zara are arranged in a line, positions 1 to 3 (left to right).

  • Maya is somewhere before Noah.
  • Zara is somewhere before Maya.
  • Zara is immediately before Maya.

Set 1.text: All Quirns are Trebs. All Trebs are Snexels. Are all Quirns definitely Snexels?
What comes next? 7, 12, 18, 25, 33, ___?
What position is Noah in?

media.html:
Maya, Felix, Noah, Zara are arranged in a line, positions 1 to 4 (left to right).

  • Felix is immediately before Zara.
  • Noah is somewhere before Maya.
  • Zara is immediately before Maya.
  • Noah is somewhere before Zara.

Set 2.text: All Blims are Wexits. All Wexits are Trebs. Are all Blims definitely Marbits?
Who is in position 3?

media.html:
Priya, Omar, Liam, Kate are arranged in a line, positions 1 to 4 (left to right).

  • Kate is immediately before Priya.
  • Kate is NOT next to Omar.
  • Omar is NOT next to Priya.
  • Liam is immediately before Kate.

What position is Zara in?

media.html:
Noah, Zara, Maya, Felix are arranged in a line, positions 1 to 4 (left to right).

  • Felix is NOT next to Zara.
  • Noah is somewhere before Maya.
  • Zara is somewhere before Noah.
  • Maya is immediately before Felix.

Set 4.text: All Zorbs are Trebs. All Trebs are Marbits. Are all Zorbs definitely Marbits?
What position is Ravi in?

media.html:
Tom, Uma, Ravi, Sofia are arranged in a line, positions 1 to 4 (left to right).

  • Sofia is NOT next to Ravi.
  • Tom is next to Sofia.
  • Uma is somewhere before Ravi.
  • Ravi is somewhere before Tom.

Who is in position 2?

media.html:
Noah, Felix, Zara, Maya are arranged in a line, positions 1 to 4 (left to right).

  • Zara is next to Noah.
  • Noah is immediately before Felix.
  • Noah is next to Felix.
  • Zara is NOT next to Maya.

Set 6.text: All Torvins are Blims. All Blims are Plimbs. Are all Torvins definitely Plimbs?
What position is Sofia in?

media.html:
Sofia, Uma, Tom, Ravi are arranged in a line, positions 1 to 4 (left to right).

  • Ravi is somewhere before Tom.
  • Tom is immediately before Uma.
  • Uma is immediately before Sofia.
  • Sofia is NOT next to Ravi.

Set 7.text: All Grondles are Snexels. All Snexels are Trebs. Are all Grondles definitely Zorbs?
Who is in position 2?

media.html:
Uma, Tom, Sofia, Ravi are arranged in a line, positions 1 to 4 (left to right).

  • Ravi is somewhere before Tom.
  • Sofia is NOT next to Ravi.
  • Ravi is somewhere before Sofia.
  • Sofia is somewhere before Uma.

Set 8.text: All Faldos are Wexits. All Wexits are Torvins. Are all Faldos definitely Torvins?
Who is in position 1?

media.html:
Kate, Liam, Omar, Priya are arranged in a line, positions 1 to 4 (left to right).

  • Kate is next to Omar.
  • Liam is next to Omar.
  • Kate is immediately before Omar.
  • Kate is somewhere before Priya.

Set 9.text: All Grondles are Blims. All Blims are Zorbs. Are all Grondles definitely Zorbs?
What position is Anya in?

media.html:
Dev, Ben, Anya, Carla are arranged in a line, positions 1 to 4 (left to right).

  • Anya is next to Dev.
  • Dev is immediately before Anya.
  • Carla is NOT next to Anya.
  • Ben is immediately before Dev.

Set 10.text: All Trebs are Quirns. All Quirns are Marbits. Are all Trebs definitely Wexits?
What comes next? 6, 8, 11, 15, 20, ___?
Who is in position 2?

media.html:
Felix, Zara, Maya, Noah are arranged in a line, positions 1 to 4 (left to right).

  • Maya is somewhere before Noah.
  • Noah is somewhere before Zara.
  • Maya is next to Felix.
  • Felix is immediately before Maya.

Set 1.text: All Zorbs are Blyphs. No Blyphs are Faldos. Can any Zorbs be Faldos?
Who is in position 4?

media.html:
Kate, Priya, Omar, Liam are arranged in a line, positions 1 to 4 (left to right).

  • Omar is NOT next to Priya.
  • Kate is in position 3.
  • Priya is next to Kate.
  • Liam is NOT next to Omar.

Set 1.text: What comes next? 2, 8, 18, 32, ___?
Column 3 is column 1 combined with column 2 by a fixed rule in every row. Which option completes the pattern?

Set 2.text: Some Marbits are Grondles. All Grondles are Quirns. Are the Marbits that are Grondles definitely Quirns?
Who is in position 4?

media.html:
Noah, Maya, Felix, Zara are arranged in a line, positions 1 to 4 (left to right).

  • Maya is NOT next to Noah.
  • Maya is somewhere before Zara.
  • Maya is somewhere between Noah and Zara.
  • Maya is next to Felix.

Set 2.text: What comes next? 8, 12, 17, 23, 30, ___?
Column 3 is column 1 combined with column 2 by a fixed rule in every row. Which option completes the pattern?

Set 3.text: Some Quirns are Marbits. All Marbits are Faldos. Are the Quirns that are Marbits definitely Faldos?
Who is in position 3?

media.html:
Dev, Anya, Ben, Carla are arranged in a line, positions 1 to 4 (left to right).

  • Anya is somewhere before Carla.
  • Anya is in position 2.
  • Dev is NOT next to Carla.
  • Carla is next to Anya.

Set 3.text: What comes next? 3, 12, 27, 48, ___?
Column 3 is column 1 combined with column 2 by a fixed rule in every row. Which option completes the pattern?

Set 4.text: All Marbits are Grondles. No Grondles are Zorbs. Can any Marbits be Zorbs?
What position is Priya in?

media.html:
Omar, Priya, Liam, Kate are arranged in a line, positions 1 to 4 (left to right).

  • Priya is immediately before Kate.
  • Omar is somewhere between Kate and Liam.
  • Kate is somewhere before Omar.
  • Liam is NOT next to Priya.

Set 5.text: Some Plimbs are Torvins. All Torvins are Blyphs. Are the Plimbs that are Torvins definitely Blyphs?
Who is in position 1?

media.html:
Tom, Ravi, Uma, Sofia are arranged in a line, positions 1 to 4 (left to right).

  • Uma is NOT next to Ravi.
  • Tom is next to Sofia.
  • Sofia is somewhere before Uma.
  • Ravi is immediately before Sofia.

Set 5.text: What comes next? 4, 16, 36, 64, ___?
Column 3 is column 1 combined with column 2 by a fixed rule in every row. Which option completes the pattern?

Set 6.text: Some Blyphs are Faldos. All Faldos are Blims. Are the Blyphs that are Faldos definitely Blims?
What position is Kate in?

media.html:
Liam, Kate, Omar, Priya are arranged in a line, positions 1 to 4 (left to right).

  • Priya is next to Liam.
  • Liam is immediately before Priya.
  • Kate is next to Priya.
  • Kate is in position 4.

Set 6.text: What comes next? 5, 7, 10, 14, 19, ___?
Column 3 is column 1 combined with column 2 by a fixed rule in every row. Which option completes the pattern?

Set 7.text: All Plimbs are Snexels. No Snexels are Quirns. Can any Plimbs be Quirns?
What position is Noah in?

media.html:
Noah, Felix, Zara, Maya are arranged in a line, positions 1 to 4 (left to right).

  • Maya is immediately before Zara.
  • Zara is next to Maya.
  • Zara is in position 3.
  • Noah is somewhere before Maya.

Set 7.text: Column 3 is column 1 combined with column 2 by a fixed rule in every row. Which option completes the pattern?

Set 8.text: Some Wexits are Grondles. All Grondles are Marbits. Are the Wexits that are Grondles definitely Marbits?
Who is in position 1?

media.html:
Omar, Priya, Kate, Liam are arranged in a line, positions 1 to 4 (left to right).

  • Omar is in position 1.
  • Priya is immediately before Kate.
  • Priya is NOT next to Omar.
  • Priya is next to Kate.

Set 8.text: What comes next? 9, 12, 16, 21, 27, ___?
Column 3 is column 1 combined with column 2 by a fixed rule in every row. Which option completes the pattern?

Set 9.text: All Wexits are Blyphs. No Blyphs are Marbits. Can any Wexits be Marbits?
Who is in position 2?

media.html:
Omar, Kate, Liam, Priya are arranged in a line, positions 1 to 4 (left to right).

  • Omar is in position 2.
  • Liam is somewhere before Priya.
  • Kate is somewhere between Omar and Priya.
  • Kate is somewhere between Priya and Liam.

Set 9.text: What comes next? 3, 6, 10, 15, 21, ___?
Column 3 is column 1 combined with column 2 by a fixed rule in every row. Which option completes the pattern?

Set 10.text: All Torvins are Grondles. No Grondles are Faldos. Can any Torvins be Faldos?
Who is in position 4?

media.html:
Zara, Noah, Felix, Maya are arranged in a line, positions 1 to 4 (left to right).

  • Felix is somewhere between Noah and Maya.
  • Zara is somewhere between Felix and Noah.
  • Felix is somewhere before Zara.
  • Felix is immediately before Zara.

Set 10.text: Column 3 is column 1 combined with column 2 by a fixed rule in every row. Which option completes the pattern?

Set 1.text: If 5x + 4y = 39 and x = 3, what is y?

Is x > 6?
(1) x and y are both positive numbers.
(2) x = 6 + 9.
options: Statement (1) ALONE is sufficient, but statement (2) alone is not
Statement (2) ALONE is sufficient, but statement (1) alone is not
BOTH statements TOGETHER are sufficient, but neither alone is sufficient
The statements together are NOT sufficient

Set 1.text:
Our competitor cut prices and their sales went up. We should cut our prices too, since that will obviously increase our sales in the same way.

What is the main flaw in this argument?
options: The argument claims the competitor's sales went down.
The argument is about hiring practices, not pricing.
The argument assumes prices can never be cut twice.
This assumes our situation is identical to the competitor's, ignoring differences in brand, market position, or why their price cut specifically worked.

Set 1.text: All Blims are Faldos. Some Faldos are Grondles. Are all Blims definitely Grondles?
Who is in position 2?

media.html:
Maya, Zara, Noah, Felix are arranged in a line, positions 1 to 4 (left to right).

  • Maya is next to Noah.
  • Noah is somewhere between Felix and Zara.
  • Zara is somewhere before Noah.
  • Felix is in position 4.
  • Noah is immediately before Felix.

Set 2.text: If 3x + 2y = 9 and x = 1, what is y?

Is x positive?
(1) x^2 = 36.
(2) |x| = 6.
options: Statement (1) ALONE is sufficient, but statement (2) alone is not
Statement (2) ALONE is sufficient, but statement (1) alone is not
BOTH statements TOGETHER are sufficient, but neither alone is sufficient
The statements together are NOT sufficient

Set 2.text:
If it rains, the game will be cancelled. The game was cancelled, so it must have rained.

What is the main flaw in this argument?
options: The argument is about a game that hasn't happened yet.
The argument assumes it never rains during games.
The argument claims the game was definitely not cancelled.
This reverses the logic of the original conditional - the game could have been cancelled for many other reasons besides rain.

Set 2.text: All Wexits are Snexels. Some Snexels are Zorbs. Are all Wexits definitely Zorbs?
What position is Kate in?

media.html:
Liam, Omar, Kate, Priya are arranged in a line, positions 1 to 4 (left to right).

  • Omar is NOT next to Kate.
  • Liam is NOT next to Priya.
  • Liam is somewhere before Kate.
  • Omar is next to Priya.
  • Kate is somewhere between Priya and Liam.

Set 3.text: If 4x + 2y = 18 and x = 2, what is y?

Is x > 5?
(1) x and y are both positive numbers.
(2) x = 5 + 3.
options: Statement (1) ALONE is sufficient, but statement (2) alone is not
Statement (2) ALONE is sufficient, but statement (1) alone is not
BOTH statements TOGETHER are sufficient, but neither alone is sufficient
The statements together are NOT sufficient

Set 3.text:
Company X's stock price rose right after they hired a new CEO with an Ivy League degree. Hiring Ivy League graduates as CEOs must be a reliable way to boost stock prices.

What is the main flaw in this argument?
options: This generalizes from a single case - one instance doesn't establish a reliable pattern, and ignores other factors that could have caused the rise.
The argument claims the CEO caused the stock price to fall.
The argument assumes the CEO was hired before the company existed.
The argument is about the company's revenue, not its stock price.

Set 3.text: All Trebs are Grondles. Some Grondles are Blyphs. Are all Trebs definitely Blyphs?
What position is Tom in?

media.html:
Ravi, Uma, Tom, Sofia are arranged in a line, positions 1 to 4 (left to right).

  • Sofia is immediately before Uma.
  • Uma is immediately before Tom.
  • Tom is NOT next to Ravi.
  • Sofia is somewhere before Uma.
  • Sofia is next to Ravi.

Is the integer N divisible by 15?
(1) N is divisible by 3.
(2) N is divisible by 5.
options: Statement (1) ALONE is sufficient, but statement (2) alone is not
Statement (2) ALONE is sufficient, but statement (1) alone is not
BOTH statements TOGETHER are sufficient, but neither alone is sufficient
The statements together are NOT sufficient

Set 4.text:
I've never gotten sick after eating at this restaurant, so their kitchen must be perfectly hygienic.

What is the main flaw in this argument?
options: This relies on personal experience as if it were comprehensive proof, ignoring that a hygiene problem might exist without having personally affected this one customer yet.
The argument claims the customer got sick every time.
The argument is about a topic unrelated to restaurants.
The argument assumes the restaurant has never served food.

Set 4.text: All Quirns are Zorbs. Some Zorbs are Wexits. Are all Quirns definitely Wexits?
What position is Maya in?

media.html:
Felix, Zara, Noah, Maya are arranged in a line, positions 1 to 4 (left to right).

  • Zara is somewhere before Felix.
  • Zara is next to Felix.
  • Noah is NOT next to Felix.
  • Maya is in position 4.
  • Felix is immediately before Maya.

Set 5.text: If 3x + 5y = 43 and x = 1, what is y?

What is the value of x?
(1) 2x + 8 = 36.
(2) x^2 = 196.
options: Statement (1) ALONE is sufficient, but statement (2) alone is not
Statement (2) ALONE is sufficient, but statement (1) alone is not
BOTH statements TOGETHER are sufficient, but neither alone is sufficient
The statements together are NOT sufficient

Set 5.text:
Our competitor cut prices and their sales went up. We should cut our prices too, since that will obviously increase our sales in the same way.

What is the main flaw in this argument?
options: This assumes our situation is identical to the competitor's, ignoring differences in brand, market position, or why their price cut specifically worked.
The argument assumes prices can never be cut twice.
The argument is about hiring practices, not pricing.
The argument claims the competitor's sales went down.

Set 5.text: All Marbits are Plimbs. Some Plimbs are Grondles. Are all Marbits definitely Grondles?
Who is in position 1?

media.html:
Noah, Maya, Zara, Felix are arranged in a line, positions 1 to 4 (left to right).

  • Maya is immediately before Noah.
  • Maya is somewhere before Zara.
  • Noah is next to Zara.
  • Maya is next to Felix.
  • Noah is in position 3.

Set 6.text: If 4x + 4y = 24 and x = 2, what is y?

What is the value of x?
(1) 2x + 6 = 24.
(2) x^2 = 81.
options: Statement (1) ALONE is sufficient, but statement (2) alone is not
Statement (2) ALONE is sufficient, but statement (1) alone is not
BOTH statements TOGETHER are sufficient, but neither alone is sufficient
The statements together are NOT sufficient

Set 6.text:
The team's new coach led them to a championship in his first year, so he is clearly a better coach than the one before him.

What is the main flaw in this argument?
options: The argument claims the team lost the championship.
The argument is about a sport that doesn't have championships.
The argument assumes the team had no players before the new coach arrived.
This ignores other factors behind the championship (player talent already in place, easier schedule, luck) and treats a single outcome as proof of the coach's individual skill.

Set 6.text: All Plimbs are Snexels. Some Snexels are Zorbs. Are all Plimbs definitely Zorbs?
Who is in position 3?

media.html:
Omar, Liam, Kate, Priya are arranged in a line, positions 1 to 4 (left to right).

  • Omar is somewhere before Priya.
  • Omar is NOT next to Liam.
  • Liam is NOT next to Priya.
  • Liam is somewhere before Omar.
  • Priya is next to Omar.

Set 7.text: Novice is to Expert as Candid is to ___?
Solve for x: 6(x + 1) = 60

Set 7.text: If 5x + 4y = 67 and x = 7, what is y?

Is the integer N divisible by 21?
(1) N is divisible by 3.
(2) N is divisible by 7.
options: Statement (1) ALONE is sufficient, but statement (2) alone is not
Statement (2) ALONE is sufficient, but statement (1) alone is not
BOTH statements TOGETHER are sufficient, but neither alone is sufficient
The statements together are NOT sufficient

Set 7.text:
Most people who bought this book gave it five stars, so it must be a well-written book.

What is the main flaw in this argument?
options: The argument is about the book's price, not its quality.
This assumes reviewers are representative of all readers, but people who leave reviews are often a self-selected group, not necessarily representative of typical readers.
The argument assumes no one has ever read the book.
The argument claims the book received mostly one-star reviews.

Set 7.text: All Plimbs are Blims. Some Blims are Grondles. Are all Plimbs definitely Grondles?
What position is Sofia in?

media.html:
Tom, Ravi, Uma, Sofia are arranged in a line, positions 1 to 4 (left to right).

  • Sofia is somewhere before Uma.
  • Tom is somewhere between Ravi and Uma.
  • Sofia is somewhere between Uma and Ravi.
  • Tom is next to Sofia.
  • Sofia is next to Uma.

Set 8.text: If 5x + 3y = 45 and x = 6, what is y?

What is the value of x?
(1) 2x + 4 = 10.
(2) x^2 = 9.
options: Statement (1) ALONE is sufficient, but statement (2) alone is not
Statement (2) ALONE is sufficient, but statement (1) alone is not
BOTH statements TOGETHER are sufficient, but neither alone is sufficient
The statements together are NOT sufficient

Set 8.text:
Either we cut the marketing budget, or the company will go bankrupt this year. Since we don't want to go bankrupt, we must cut the marketing budget.

What is the main flaw in this argument?
options: The argument claims cutting the budget guarantees success.
The argument never mentions bankruptcy as a possibility.
The argument assumes the marketing budget is currently zero.
This is a false dichotomy - it presents only two options when there may be other ways to avoid bankruptcy.

Set 8.text: All Torvins are Trebs. Some Trebs are Blims. Are all Torvins definitely Blims?
Who is in position 2?

media.html:
Anya, Ben, Dev, Carla are arranged in a line, positions 1 to 4 (left to right).

  • Anya is immediately before Dev.
  • Ben is in position 1.
  • Ben is somewhere before Carla.
  • Carla is somewhere before Dev.
  • Anya is next to Carla.

Set 9.text: If 2x + 4y = 54 and x = 7, what is y?

Is x > 12?
(1) x and y are both positive numbers.
(2) x = 12 + 2.
options: Statement (1) ALONE is sufficient, but statement (2) alone is not
Statement (2) ALONE is sufficient, but statement (1) alone is not
BOTH statements TOGETHER are sufficient, but neither alone is sufficient
The statements together are NOT sufficient

Set 9.text:
I've never gotten sick after eating at this restaurant, so their kitchen must be perfectly hygienic.

What is the main flaw in this argument?
options: The argument assumes the restaurant has never served food.
The argument claims the customer got sick every time.
This relies on personal experience as if it were comprehensive proof, ignoring that a hygiene problem might exist without having personally affected this one customer yet.
The argument is about a topic unrelated to restaurants.

Set 9.text: All Grondles are Marbits. Some Marbits are Snexels. Are all Grondles definitely Snexels?
Who is in position 1?

media.html:
Zara, Noah, Felix, Maya are arranged in a line, positions 1 to 4 (left to right).

  • Noah is in position 2.
  • Zara is somewhere before Felix.
  • Maya is next to Felix.
  • Felix is next to Noah.
  • Maya is NOT next to Noah.

Set 10.text: If 2x + 3y = 32 and x = 4, what is y?

options: Statement (1) ALONE is sufficient, but statement (2) alone is not
Statement (2) ALONE is sufficient, but statement (1) alone is not
BOTH statements TOGETHER are sufficient, but neither alone is sufficient
The statements together are NOT sufficient

Set 10.text:
The new employee arrived late on her first day. This shows she has a poor work ethic and will likely underperform throughout her time at the company.

What is the main flaw in this argument?
options: The argument claims the employee was never late again.
The argument assumes the employee works in a different department.
The argument is actually praising the new employee's punctuality.
This is a hasty generalization - a single instance is used to draw a sweeping conclusion about her overall character and future performance.

Set 10.text: All Snexels are Grondles. Some Grondles are Marbits. Are all Snexels definitely Marbits?
Who is in position 1?

media.html:
Liam, Omar, Priya, Kate are arranged in a line, positions 1 to 4 (left to right).

  • Omar is next to Kate.
  • Priya is NOT next to Omar.
  • Kate is NOT next to Priya.
  • Liam is next to Kate.
  • Priya is somewhere before Omar.

Set 1.text:
This diet must be effective because everyone who has tried it and succeeded says it works.

What is the main flaw in this argument?
options: The argument confuses the diet with a form of exercise.
The argument assumes diets never work.
This is circular reasoning - it only counts the opinions of people for whom the diet worked, guaranteeing a biased conclusion regardless of the diet's actual effectiveness.
The argument is only about one specific person's experience.

Set 1.text: What position is Tom in?

media.html:
Uma, Sofia, Tom, Ravi are arranged in a line, positions 1 to 4 (left to right).

  • Tom is somewhere before Sofia.
  • Uma is immediately before Ravi.
  • Tom is next to Sofia.
  • Ravi is NOT next to Tom.

Set 3.text:
Since the new bridge was built, traffic accidents in the city have decreased by 20%. Clearly, the bridge design is responsible for making the roads safer.

What is the main flaw in this argument?
options: The argument ignores other possible causes - other changes could explain the decrease, not necessarily the bridge itself.
The argument claims accidents increased when they actually decreased.
The 20% figure is stated as a percentage instead of a raw number.
The argument assumes the bridge was expensive to build.

Set 4.text:
I've never gotten sick after eating at this restaurant, so their kitchen must be perfectly hygienic.

What is the main flaw in this argument?
options: The argument assumes the restaurant has never served food.
This relies on personal experience as if it were comprehensive proof, ignoring that a hygiene problem might exist without having personally affected this one customer yet.
The argument claims the customer got sick every time.
The argument is about a topic unrelated to restaurants.

media.html:
Dev, Ben, Carla, Anya are arranged in a line, positions 1 to 4 (left to right).

  • Ben is next to Dev.
  • Dev is immediately before Carla.
  • Anya is somewhere before Dev.
  • Carla is next to Dev.

Set 5.text:
This diet must be effective because everyone who has tried it and succeeded says it works.

What is the main flaw in this argument?
options: The argument confuses the diet with a form of exercise.
This is circular reasoning - it only counts the opinions of people for whom the diet worked, guaranteeing a biased conclusion regardless of the diet's actual effectiveness.
The argument is only about one specific person's experience.
The argument assumes diets never work.

Set 5.text: What position is Kate in?

media.html:
Liam, Priya, Omar, Kate are arranged in a line, positions 1 to 4 (left to right).

  • Priya is next to Liam.
  • Kate is somewhere before Priya.
  • Priya is immediately before Omar.
  • Kate is somewhere before Liam.

Set 6.text:
Our competitor cut prices and their sales went up. We should cut our prices too, since that will obviously increase our sales in the same way.

What is the main flaw in this argument?
options: The argument claims the competitor's sales went down.
This assumes our situation is identical to the competitor's, ignoring differences in brand, market position, or why their price cut specifically worked.
The argument assumes prices can never be cut twice.
The argument is about hiring practices, not pricing.

Set 6.text: Who is in position 3?

media.html:
Tom, Uma, Ravi, Sofia are arranged in a line, positions 1 to 4 (left to right).

  • Ravi is NOT next to Sofia.
  • Tom is next to Sofia.
  • Uma is immediately before Sofia.
  • Uma is NOT next to Ravi.

Set 7.text:
A survey of 50 gym members found that people who work out in the morning report higher energy levels than those who work out in the evening. Therefore, working out in the morning causes higher energy levels.

What is the main flaw in this argument?
options: The sample size of 50 is too small to survey anyone.
The argument mistakes correlation for causation - people with naturally higher energy might simply prefer morning workouts, rather than mornings workouts causing the energy.
The argument assumes all gym members exercise every day.
The argument contradicts itself about when people work out.

Set 8.text:
Every successful entrepreneur I've read about dropped out of college. Therefore, dropping out of college is a good strategy for becoming a successful entrepreneur.

What is the main flaw in this argument?
options: This is an unrepresentative sample - it only looks at entrepreneurs who succeeded and ignores the much larger number of college dropouts who did not become successful entrepreneurs.
The argument claims college dropouts always fail.
The argument assumes all entrepreneurs attended college.
The argument is about a topic unrelated to entrepreneurship.

  • Kate is somewhere before Omar.
  • Liam is immediately before Omar.

Set 9.text:
The team's new coach led them to a championship in his first year, so he is clearly a better coach than the one before him.

What is the main flaw in this argument?
options: The argument claims the team lost the championship.
The argument assumes the team had no players before the new coach arrived.
The argument is about a sport that doesn't have championships.
This ignores other factors behind the championship (player talent already in place, easier schedule, luck) and treats a single outcome as proof of the coach's individual skill.

Set 9.text: What position is Ben in?

media.html:
Ben, Carla, Dev, Anya are arranged in a line, positions 1 to 4 (left to right).

  • Anya is NOT next to Carla.
  • Carla is somewhere before Anya.
  • Ben is next to Dev.
  • Carla is NOT next to Dev.

Either we cut the marketing budget, or the company will go bankrupt this year. Since we don't want to go bankrupt, we must cut the marketing budget.

What is the main flaw in this argument?
options: The argument assumes the marketing budget is currently zero.
The argument claims cutting the budget guarantees success.
This is a false dichotomy - it presents only two options when there may be other ways to avoid bankruptcy.
The argument never mentions bankruptcy as a possibility.

Set 10.text: Who is in position 4?

media.html:
Tom, Ravi, Sofia, Uma are arranged in a line, positions 1 to 4 (left to right).

  • Ravi is immediately before Tom.
  • Sofia is somewhere before Uma.
  • Tom is somewhere before Sofia.
  • Uma is NOT next to Tom.

Set 1.text: Statement: "The north region had the highest sales of any region in 2023." - Is this True, False, or Cannot Say based only on the passage?

media.text: Ridgeline Consulting's revenue grew from $55M in 2022 to $64.9M in 2023, an increase of 18%. Headcount shrank from 537 to 473 employees over the same period. The north region generated the highest sales of any region in 2023.

Set 1.text: Statement: "Ridgeline Consulting's revenue in 2023 was lower than in 2022." - Is this True, False, or Cannot Say based only on the passage?

media.text: Ridgeline Consulting's revenue grew from $66M in 2022 to $78.5M in 2023, an increase of 19%. Headcount shrank from 262 to 231 employees over the same period. The east region generated the highest sales of any region in 2023.

Set 1.text:
The city installed more streetlights last year, and crime rates dropped. Therefore, if we install streetlights everywhere, crime will disappear entirely.

What is the main flaw in this argument?
options: The argument assumes streetlights were never installed at all.
The argument claims crime rates actually increased.
This extrapolates a partial, moderate effect into an absolute claim, assuming the same relationship would scale indefinitely and eliminate crime completely.
The argument is only about property crime, not violent crime.

Is N even?

(2) N + 1 = 13, which is odd.

Set 1.text: If 3x + 4y = 15 and x = 1, what is y?
What comes next? 3, 6, 15, 30, 51, ___?
All Zorbs are Marbits. Some Marbits are Torvins. Are all Zorbs definitely Torvins?
What position is Priya in?

media.html:
Liam, Priya, Nadia, Kate, Omar are arranged in a line, positions 1 to 5 (left to right).

  • Priya is somewhere before Kate.
  • Kate is NOT next to Priya.
  • Omar is immediately before Kate.
  • Liam is immediately before Nadia.
  • Priya is next to Nadia.

Set 2.text: Statement: "Prime Analytics's revenue in 2023 was higher than in 2022." - Is this True, False, or Cannot Say based only on the passage?

media.text: Prime Analytics's revenue grew from $81M in 2022 to $89.1M in 2023, an increase of 10%. Headcount shrank from 227 to 204 employees over the same period. The north region generated the highest sales of any region in 2023.

Set 2.text: Statement: "Solara Energy's revenue in 2023 was higher than in 2022." - Is this True, False, or Cannot Say based only on the passage?

media.text: Solara Energy's revenue grew from $83M in 2022 to $100.4M in 2023, an increase of 21%. Headcount shrank from 483 to 415 employees over the same period. The west region generated the highest sales of any region in 2023.

Set 2.text:
The team's new coach led them to a championship in his first year, so he is clearly a better coach than the one before him.

What is the main flaw in this argument?
options: The argument is about a sport that doesn't have championships.
This ignores other factors behind the championship (player talent already in place, easier schedule, luck) and treats a single outcome as proof of the coach's individual skill.
The argument assumes the team had no players before the new coach arrived.
The argument claims the team lost the championship.

media.html:
Maya, Noah, Zara, Ines, Felix are arranged in a line, positions 1 to 5 (left to right).

  • Maya is in position 3.
  • Zara is NOT next to Maya.
  • Zara is somewhere before Ines.
  • Ines is somewhere between Felix and Noah.
  • Ines is somewhere between Zara and Felix.

Set 2.text: The number of shapes follows an arithmetic rule across each row. Which option completes the pattern?

Set 3.text: Statement: "The east region had the highest sales of any region in 2023." - Is this True, False, or Cannot Say based only on the passage?

media.text: Bellmark Insurance's revenue grew from $59M in 2022 to $73.2M in 2023, an increase of 24%. Headcount grew from 397 to 441 employees over the same period. The east region generated the highest sales of any region in 2023.

Set 3.text: Statement: "The west region had the highest sales of any region in 2023." - Is this True, False, or Cannot Say based only on the passage?

media.text: Verdant Foods's revenue grew from $85M in 2022 to $99.4M in 2023, an increase of 17%. Headcount grew from 569 to 632 employees over the same period. The north region generated the highest sales of any region in 2023.

Set 3.text:
Most people who bought this book gave it five stars, so it must be a well-written book.

What is the main flaw in this argument?
options: The argument claims the book received mostly one-star reviews.
The argument is about the book's price, not its quality.
This assumes reviewers are representative of all readers, but people who leave reviews are often a self-selected group, not necessarily representative of typical readers.
The argument assumes no one has ever read the book.

Set 3.text: If 5x + 4y = 27 and x = 3, what is y?
What comes next? 2, 5, 14, 29, 50, ___?
All Plimbs are Zorbs. Some Zorbs are Torvins. Are all Plimbs definitely Torvins?
Who is in position 5?

media.html:
Nadia, Kate, Liam, Priya, Omar are arranged in a line, positions 1 to 5 (left to right).

  • Kate is immediately before Omar.
  • Nadia is next to Liam.
  • Nadia is somewhere before Priya.
  • Omar is immediately before Nadia.
  • Nadia is somewhere between Liam and Omar.

Set 4.text: Statement: "Verdant Foods employed fewer people in 2023 than in 2022." - Is this True, False, or Cannot Say based only on the passage?

media.text: Verdant Foods's revenue grew from $58M in 2022 to $70.8M in 2023, an increase of 22%. Headcount shrank from 534 to 486 employees over the same period. The south region generated the highest sales of any region in 2023.

Set 4.text: Statement: "Verdant Foods's profit margin improved between 2022 and 2023." - Is this True, False, or Cannot Say based only on the passage?

media.text: Verdant Foods's revenue grew from $49M in 2022 to $58.8M in 2023, an increase of 20%. Headcount grew from 550 to 632 employees over the same period. The west region generated the highest sales of any region in 2023.

Set 4.text:
Since the new bridge was built, traffic accidents in the city have decreased by 20%. Clearly, the bridge design is responsible for making the roads safer.

What is the main flaw in this argument?
options: The 20% figure is stated as a percentage instead of a raw number.
The argument ignores other possible causes - other changes could explain the decrease, not necessarily the bridge itself.
The argument assumes the bridge was expensive to build.
The argument claims accidents increased when they actually decreased.

What comes next? 1, 3, 9, 19, 33, ___?
All Snexels are Plimbs. Some Plimbs are Marbits. Are all Snexels definitely Marbits?
What position is Nadia in?

media.html:
Kate, Nadia, Omar, Liam, Priya are arranged in a line, positions 1 to 5 (left to right).

  • Priya is NOT next to Omar.
  • Nadia is next to Liam.
  • Liam is in position 5.
  • Omar is NOT next to Liam.
  • Kate is somewhere between Omar and Liam.

Set 4.text: The number of shapes follows an arithmetic rule across each row. Which option completes the pattern?

Set 5.text: Statement: "The north region had higher sales than the South region by more than double." - Is this True, False, or Cannot Say based only on the passage?

media.text: Ridgeline Consulting's revenue grew from $84M in 2022 to $94.9M in 2023, an increase of 13%. Headcount grew from 597 to 633 employees over the same period. The north region generated the highest sales of any region in 2023.

Set 5.text: Statement: "Bellmark Insurance was more profitable than its competitors in 2023." - Is this True, False, or Cannot Say based only on the passage?

media.text: Bellmark Insurance's revenue grew from $63M in 2022 to $67.4M in 2023, an increase of 7%. Headcount shrank from 561 to 482 employees over the same period. The west region generated the highest sales of any region in 2023.

Set 5.text:
If it rains, the game will be cancelled. The game was cancelled, so it must have rained.

What is the main flaw in this argument?
options: This reverses the logic of the original conditional - the game could have been cancelled for many other reasons besides rain.
The argument assumes it never rains during games.
The argument is about a game that hasn't happened yet.
The argument claims the game was definitely not cancelled.

Statement (2) ALONE is sufficient, but statement (1) alone is not

Set 5.text: If 4x + 4y = 32 and x = 1, what is y?
What comes next? 1, 3, 9, 19, 33, ___?
All Blyphs are Wexits. Some Wexits are Quirns. Are all Blyphs definitely Quirns?
What position is Maya in?

media.html:
Maya, Noah, Ines, Felix, Zara are arranged in a line, positions 1 to 5 (left to right).

  • Maya is immediately before Felix.
  • Ines is somewhere between Maya and Zara.
  • Noah is somewhere between Ines and Zara.
  • Noah is NOT next to Felix.
  • Ines is NOT next to Felix.

Set 6.text: Statement: "Verdant Foods's revenue in 2023 was higher than in 2022." - Is this True, False, or Cannot Say based only on the passage?

media.text: Verdant Foods's revenue grew from $76M in 2022 to $90.4M in 2023, an increase of 19%. Headcount shrank from 338 to 291 employees over the same period. The east region generated the highest sales of any region in 2023.

Set 6.text: Statement: "Ashgrove Manufacturing's revenue in 2023 was lower than in 2022." - Is this True, False, or Cannot Say based only on the passage?

media.text: Ashgrove Manufacturing's revenue grew from $64M in 2022 to $67.2M in 2023, an increase of 5%. Headcount shrank from 280 to 263 employees over the same period. The north region generated the highest sales of any region in 2023.

Set 6.text:
Sales increased in the same month we redesigned our logo. The new logo must be driving sales.

What is the main flaw in this argument?
options: The argument claims sales decreased after the redesign.
The argument assumes the company has never had a logo before.
This assumes the redesign caused the sales increase without ruling out other events happening in the same period (seasonality, promotions, market trends) that could equally explain it.
The argument is only about the cost of the redesign.

What comes next? 3, 6, 15, 30, 51, ___?
All Faldos are Wexits. Some Wexits are Grondles. Are all Faldos definitely Grondles?
What position is Omar in?

media.html:
Nadia, Liam, Omar, Priya, Kate are arranged in a line, positions 1 to 5 (left to right).

  • Priya is immediately before Kate.
  • Priya is NOT next to Liam.
  • Priya is somewhere between Liam and Omar.
  • Kate is NOT next to Omar.
  • Omar is somewhere between Nadia and Liam.

Set 7.text: Statement: "The south region had the highest sales of any region in 2023." - Is this True, False, or Cannot Say based only on the passage?

media.text: Ridgeline Consulting's revenue grew from $48M in 2022 to $50.9M in 2023, an increase of 6%. Headcount grew from 251 to 266 employees over the same period. The north region generated the highest sales of any region in 2023.

Set 7.text: Statement: "Cobalt Logistics's revenue in 2023 was lower than in 2022." - Is this True, False, or Cannot Say based only on the passage?

media.text: Cobalt Logistics's revenue grew from $40M in 2022 to $44.0M in 2023, an increase of 10%. Headcount shrank from 424 to 394 employees over the same period. The north region generated the highest sales of any region in 2023.

Set 7.text:
The committee rejected the proposal because it was submitted by a first-year employee, and first-year employees don't have enough experience to make good proposals.

What is the main flaw in this argument?
options: The argument assumes the committee has no first-year employees.
The argument is about a proposal that doesn't actually exist.
This dismisses the proposal based on its source rather than evaluating its actual merits - a source-based dismissal rather than a substantive critique.
The argument claims the proposal was accepted, not rejected.

All Snexels are Blims. Some Blims are Torvins. Are all Snexels definitely Torvins?
Who is in position 3?

media.html:
Kate, Nadia, Omar, Liam, Priya are arranged in a line, positions 1 to 5 (left to right).

  • Omar is somewhere before Nadia.
  • Liam is immediately before Nadia.
  • Priya is somewhere between Kate and Liam.
  • Priya is somewhere between Omar and Kate.
  • Omar is somewhere before Kate.

Set 7.text: The number of shapes follows an arithmetic rule across each row. Which option completes the pattern?

Set 8.text: Statement: "Solara Energy employed more people in 2023 than in 2022." - Is this True, False, or Cannot Say based only on the passage?

media.text: Solara Energy's revenue grew from $48M in 2022 to $57.6M in 2023, an increase of 20%. Headcount shrank from 556 to 517 employees over the same period. The east region generated the highest sales of any region in 2023.

Set 8.text: Statement: "Northfield Retail employed fewer people in 2023 than in 2022." - Is this True, False, or Cannot Say based only on the passage?

media.text: Northfield Retail's revenue grew from $88M in 2022 to $98.6M in 2023, an increase of 12%. Headcount grew from 240 to 274 employees over the same period. The north region generated the highest sales of any region in 2023.

Set 8.text:
Sales increased in the same month we redesigned our logo. The new logo must be driving sales.

What is the main flaw in this argument?
options: The argument assumes the company has never had a logo before.
This assumes the redesign caused the sales increase without ruling out other events happening in the same period (seasonality, promotions, market trends) that could equally explain it.
The argument is only about the cost of the redesign.
The argument claims sales decreased after the redesign.

Set 8.text: If 4x + 5y = 39 and x = 6, what is y?
What comes next? 1, 2, 5, 10, 17, ___?
All Grondles are Quirns. Some Quirns are Blims. Are all Grondles definitely Blims?
Who is in position 2?

media.html:
Ines, Zara, Noah, Maya, Felix are arranged in a line, positions 1 to 5 (left to right).

  • Noah is immediately before Ines.
  • Ines is NOT next to Maya.
  • Zara is somewhere before Felix.
  • Maya is immediately before Zara.
  • Felix is immediately before Noah.

Set 9.text: Statement: "The west region had the highest sales of any region in 2023." - Is this True, False, or Cannot Say based only on the passage?

media.text: Verdant Foods's revenue grew from $46M in 2022 to $54.3M in 2023, an increase of 18%. Headcount grew from 563 to 608 employees over the same period. The north region generated the highest sales of any region in 2023.

Set 9.text: Statement: "Bellmark Insurance employed fewer people in 2023 than in 2022." - Is this True, False, or Cannot Say based only on the passage?

media.text: Bellmark Insurance's revenue grew from $78M in 2022 to $94.4M in 2023, an increase of 21%. Headcount grew from 295 to 324 employees over the same period. The north region generated the highest sales of any region in 2023.

Set 9.text:
Since the new bridge was built, traffic accidents in the city have decreased by 20%. Clearly, the bridge design is responsible for making the roads safer.

What is the main flaw in this argument?
options: The argument ignores other possible causes - other changes could explain the decrease, not necessarily the bridge itself.
The 20% figure is stated as a percentage instead of a raw number.
The argument assumes the bridge was expensive to build.
The argument claims accidents increased when they actually decreased.

What position is Carla in?

media.html:
Ben, Elin, Carla, Anya, Dev are arranged in a line, positions 1 to 5 (left to right).

  • Carla is immediately before Dev.
  • Anya is next to Carla.
  • Dev is somewhere between Carla and Elin.
  • Carla is somewhere before Elin.
  • Ben is in position 5.

Set 10.text: Statement: "The east region had the highest sales of any region in 2023." - Is this True, False, or Cannot Say based only on the passage?

media.text: Verdant Foods's revenue grew from $42M in 2022 to $47.0M in 2023, an increase of 12%. Headcount shrank from 288 to 253 employees over the same period. The east region generated the highest sales of any region in 2023.

Set 10.text: Statement: "Prime Analytics employed fewer people in 2023 than in 2022." - Is this True, False, or Cannot Say based only on the passage?

media.text: Prime Analytics's revenue grew from $78M in 2022 to $85.0M in 2023, an increase of 9%. Headcount shrank from 253 to 240 employees over the same period. The south region generated the highest sales of any region in 2023.

The argument assumes the employee works in a different department.
The argument claims the employee was never late again.
The argument is actually praising the new employee's punctuality.

All Zorbs are Snexels. Some Snexels are Trebs. Are all Zorbs definitely Trebs?
What position is Nadia in?

media.html:
Nadia, Omar, Liam, Kate, Priya are arranged in a line, positions 1 to 5 (left to right).

  • Omar is immediately before Priya.
  • Nadia is NOT next to Liam.
  • Liam is somewhere before Priya.
  • Liam is in position 1.
  • Omar is in position 3.
